title: draba altaica var. modesta
intro: Draba altaica var. modesta, also known as Siberian Whitlowgrass, is a rare and endangered flowering plant that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). Its medicinal properties are attributed to its roots and leaves, which contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Siberian Whitlowgrass include its potential to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Siberian Whitlowgrass can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is traditionally used in folk medicine in Russia and other parts of Asia, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
